Party_Details.Name: Worldwide Convention Planners Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Worldwide Convention Planners Pte Ltd was incorporated to organize conventions and events
The plaintiff purchased the property at 51A/B Neil Road for $2,400,000
A valuation report valued the property at $2,050,000
On 2006-08-30, the first defendant and Mr Gilbert Goh discussed and resolved that the plaintiff would pay $42,000 to the first defendant as director’s fees
On 2006-08-30, the first defendant and Mr Gilbert Goh discussed and resolved that the plaintiff would pay $58,200 to Your Holiday Planners Pte Ltd
On 2006-08-30, the first defendant and Mr Gilbert Goh discussed and resolved that the plaintiff would distribute $510,000 among the shareholders

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the directors of a company can be held legally responsible for actions that allegedly contravened their management obligations
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must act in the best interest of the company and ensure that all transactions are conducted with transparency and proper authorization
Party_Details.Application: The first defendant acted without proper board approval in selling the property and distributing funds. all actions were ratified by the shareholders
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the plaintiff's claims were unsubstantiated and that the transactions were ratified by the shareholders.

Party_Details.Name: Lee Khiam Long

Facts.Text: Lee Khiam Long was the managing director of Worldwide Convention Planners Pte Ltd
On 2006-08-30, Lee Khiam Long participated in a meeting that resolved to pay him $42,000 as director's fees
On 2006-08-30, Lee Khiam Long participated in a meeting that resolved to sell the property to the second and third defendants and to divide $510,000 among the shareholders
The sale of the property to the second defendant and his son, the third defendant, completed on 2007-01-29

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director can be held legally responsible for actions taken in the management of company assets without explicit board approval
Party_Details.Rule: A director must ensure that all significant company transactions are approved by the board or shareholders to avoid contravening management obligations
Party_Details.Application: The sale and distribution of funds were approved by the shareholders. these actions were unauthorized
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court concluded that the actions were ratified by the shareholders and dismissed the claims against the defendant.

Party_Details.Name: Lee Khiam Long
Party_Details.Issue: Whether Lee Khiam Long's counterclaim for $111,122.25 should be allowed
Party_Details.Rule: The counterclaim was supported by documentary evidence that was not challenged
Party_Details.Application: No officer from the plaintiff gave evidence to challenge Lee Khiam Long's counterclaim, and the documentary evidence adduced by him was not challenged
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court granted judgment on Lee Khiam Long's counterclaim for $111,122.25 because no officer from the plaintiff gave evidence to challenge it and the documentary evidence adduced by him was not challenged.

Party_Details.Name: Worldwide Convention Planners Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Worldwide Convention Planners Pte Ltd was claimed against for $111,122.25 by Lee Khiam Long.

Party_Details.Rule: A company is obligated to reimburse directors for legitimate expenses incurred in the course of their duties
Party_Details.Application: The defendant by counterclaim did not provide evidence to refute the claim. The plaintiff by counterclaim provided documentation supporting the expenses
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found the claim to be valid and ordered the company to reimburse the claimed amount.
